document.title: Digital Radiography System Overview: CXDI-40G / CXDI-40C / CXDI-50G / CXDI-31 / DR Image Viewer

sourceSummary.oneLine: Canon catalogue describing Digital Radiography systems CXDI-40G, CXDI-40C, CXDI-50G, CXDI-31, and DR Image Viewer.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Manufacturer product catalogue and system overview for Canon Digital Radiography systems, including specifications, applications, workflow, connectivity, and configuration information.
keyPoints: Canon identifies CXDI-31 as the lightest and most compact DR system of its kind.
CXDI-31 is described for trauma, neonatal, orthopedic, skull, neck, shoulder, limb, and extremity imaging applications.
CXDI-31 specifications include GOS scintillator, LANMIT 2 sensor, 9 x 11 inch imaging area, 6.5 million pixels, 100 micron pixel size, 4,096 grayscale 12-bit output, and DICOM 3.0 connectivity.
Canon DR systems use exclusive Flat Panel Detector technology and centralized Canon Control Station workflow.
DICOM 3.0 compliance is stated for image transfer to servers using Storage Service Class SCU and printers using Print Management Service Class SCU.

concepts.evidence: LANMIX is a new name that represents the total solution offered by Canon, through the combination of our LANMIT (Large Area New-MIS Sensor and TFT) detector and other X-ray imaging equipment and software.

evidence.claim: The document is a Canon Digital Radiography system overview for CXDI-40G, CXDI-40C, CXDI-50G, CXDI-31, and DR Image Viewer.
evidence.evidenceText: Digital Radiography SYSTEM OVERVIEW CXDI-40G / CXDI-40C / CXDI-50G / CXDI-31 / DR Image Viewer

evidence.claim: The catalogue identifies Canon Inc. as copyright holder and includes code 0037W464 and print code 0703AB00.
evidence.evidenceText: Code: 0037W464 © CANON INC. 2003 0703AB00 PRINTED IN JAPAN

evidence.claim: CXDI-31 is described as the lightest and most compact DR system of its kind.
evidence.evidenceText: The Lightest & Most Compact DR System of Its Kind

evidence.claim: CXDI-31 is described as used for trauma, neonatal, and orthopedic imaging.
evidence.evidenceText: Many institutions have adopted the CXDI-31 as their preferred system for trauma, neonatal, and orthopedic imaging.

evidence.claim: CXDI-31 is described as suitable for lateral and axial imaging of skull, neck, shoulder, limbs, and extremities.
evidence.evidenceText: Its compact size makes it easy to position, and simplifies lateral and axial imaging of skull, neck, shoulder, limbs, and extremities.

evidence.claim: CXDI-31 uses a GOS scintillator and LANMIT 2 sensor.
CXDI-31 has a 9 x 11 inch imaging area, equivalent to 22.6 x 28.8 cm.
evidence.evidenceText: Imaging area: 9 x 11 inches (22.6 x 28.8 cm)

evidence.claim: CXDI-31 resolution is stated as 6.5 million pixels with each pixel 100 microns.
CXDI-31 image output is stated as 4,096 grayscale, 12-bit.
CXDI-31 preview images are available in approximately 3 seconds after x-ray exposure.
evidence.evidenceText: Preview images in approx. 3 seconds after x-ray exposure

evidence.claim: Canon DR systems capture images using an exclusive Flat Panel Detector.
evidence.evidenceText: Each Canon DR system captures images using an exclusive Flat Panel Detector.

evidence.claim: Canon Control Station centralizes operations from X-ray emission to image confirmation and final distribution.
evidence.evidenceText: all operations, from X-ray emission to image confirmation to final distribution, are controlled from a centralized location, the Canon Control Station.

evidence.claim: Canon Control Station offers temporary storage of over 700 images.
evidence.evidenceText: the Canon Control Station offers temporary storage of over 700 images.

evidence.claim: The DR Image Viewer is designed for examining images captured with Canon DR systems.
evidence.evidenceText: Designed for optimum use with Digital Radiography, the Canon DR Image Viewer offers the best way to examine images captured with your Canon DR system.

evidence.claim: The DR Image Viewer can send images to an industry standard PACS.
evidence.evidenceText: It provides pixel-to-pixel display of high-resolution images, multiple image display for comparing studies, and the ability to send images to any industry standard PACS.

evidence.claim: The document states DICOM 3.0 compliance for sending X-ray images to servers using Storage Service Class SCU and printers using Print Management Service Class SCU.
evidence.evidenceText: DICOM 3.0 compliant. X-ray images are sent to servers using Storage Service Class (SCU) and to printers using Print Management Service Class (SCU).

evidence.claim: Options are available for HIS/RIS communication and non-DICOM communication.
evidence.evidenceText: Options are available for HIS/RIS communication and non-DICOM communication.

evidence.claim: Specifications are subject to change without notice.
evidence.evidenceText: Specifications are subject to change without notice.

reviewFlags.reason: No explicit regulatory authority or jurisdiction is stated in the source text; FDA appears only in metadata as authoritySignal and was not used as a document authority.
No explicit document date is stated; the visible 2003 text is a copyright line, not clearly a document date.
Revision/version is not explicitly labeled; code 0037W464 and print code 0703AB00 were captured as available document identifiers.
Several specification-table lines show low OCR quality with corrupted characters, so only clearly readable claims were extracted.
